C7-011.source_text:
7-2.10 REFERENCE LINE. When required, references must bear directly on the subject of the letter. List references in the order they appear in the text. Always mention cited references in the text. Never list the same item in both the reference and enclosure lines. If a reference is not held by all addressees, annotate the reference with the appropriate NOTAL statement. On copies only, not the original, annotate references with "w/ref (x)" where x is the reference letter.

Format the reference line as follows:
- Place "Ref:" on the second line below the subject line (one blank line between Subject and Ref:)
- Follow "Ref:" with four spaces
- Use lowercase letters in parentheses: (a), (b), (c), ..., (z), (aa), (ab), (ac), etc.
- Continuation lines align under the first word after "Ref:    "

Reference formats:
1. Naval correspondence: (a)  COMNAVAIRFORINST 5216.5A
2. Business letters: (b)  Smith Industries letter Ser 123/45 of 15 Mar 2025
3. E-mails: (c)  John Smith e-mail of 15 Mar 2025
4. Messages: (d)  COMNAVAIRLANT message 151000Z Mar 2025
5. Endorsements: (e)  NAVSUP 5216/123 of 15 Mar 2025
6. PHONCON/Mtg: (f)  PHONCON with Mr. John Smith on 15 Mar 2025 / (g)  Mtg with Mr. John Smith on 15 Mar 2025
7. Instructions: (h)  SECNAVINST 5216.5A
8. Notices: (i)  SECNAVNOTICE 5216
9. DoD directives: (j)  DoDD 5216.5
10. Forms: (k)  SECNAV Form 5216/1
11. Reports: (l)  Report of 15 Mar 2025
12. Publications: (m)  Navy Correspondence Manual
13. CFR: (n)  32 CFR 700
14. FR: (o)  89 FR 12345
15. U.S.C.: (p)  10 U.S.C. 7001
16. E.O.: (q)  E.O. 12345
17. My usage: (r)  My message of 15 Mar 2025
18. Your usage: (s)  Your message of 15 Mar 2025
C7-011.plain_language_rule: When references are required, they must directly relate to the letter's subject. List references in the exact order they first appear in the body text, assigning sequential markers (a), (b), (c), etc. as they appear. After (z), continue with (aa), (ab), (ac), and so on. Every reference listed must be explicitly mentioned in the text. The same item cannot appear in both reference and enclosure lines. Format: 'Ref:' on the second line below Subject (one blank line gap), followed by four spaces, then the reference markers. Continuation lines align under the first character after 'Ref:    '. If a reference is not held by all addressees, add a NOTAL annotation. On copies only (not the original), add 'w/ref (x)' where x is the reference letter.
C7-011.notes: Critical placement: Ref: appears on the second line below Subject with exactly one blank line between them. Spacing: exactly four spaces after 'Ref:' before the first marker. Marker sequencing: lowercase letters in parentheses, assigned sequentially as references appear in text (not alphabetically sorted by title). After (z) comes (aa), (ab), etc. Continuation lines align under the first word after the heading. Content requirement: 'Always mention cited references in the text' means every listed reference must be explicitly cited in the body. Ordering rule: 'List references in the order they appear in the text' means order of first mention in body text, with markers assigned sequentially. The alphabetical sequence (a), (b), (c)... is the marker system, not a sorting criterion. Enclosure exception: never duplicate an item in both reference and enclosure lines. NOTAL handling: annotate references not held by all addressees. w/ref (x) notation: appears on copies only, never on the original.
